title: Meridian UK Gilt Fund
content:
The Meridian UK Gilt Fund is a conservative fixed income investment vehicle designed to provide capital preservation and steady income generation through exposure to UK government securities. As at 31 January 2025, the fund maintains total assets under management of £847.3 million, positioning it as one of our flagship offerings for risk-averse investors seeking sterling-denominated fixed income exposure.

Fund Composition and Strategy
The portfolio comprises 78% conventional UK gilts with maturities ranging from 2 to 30 years, 15% index-linked gilts providing inflation protection, and 7% Treasury bills for liquidity management. Our average portfolio duration stands at 8.2 years, with a gross redemption yield of 3.87% as at month-end. The fund's modified duration of 7.8 indicates moderate interest rate sensitivity, appropriate for the current monetary policy environment.

Performance Metrics
Over the trailing twelve months, the fund has delivered a total return of 4.23%, outperforming the FTSE Actuaries Government Securities All Stocks Index by 67 basis points after fees. The fund's annual management charge of 0.45% remains competitive within the gilt fund universe, with total expense ratio of 0.52%. Dividend distributions are made quarterly, with the most recent distribution of 0.87p per unit paid on 15 January 2025.

Risk Characteristics
In accordance with FCA COBS 9.2 requirements regarding appropriateness assessments, we classify this product as suitable for investors with basic investment knowledge and low risk tolerance. The fund exhibits annualised volatility of 6.3% over three years, significantly lower than broader fixed income indices. Maximum drawdown during the 2022 gilt crisis was limited to -18.7%, demonstrating the fund's resilience during periods of market stress.

Regulatory Compliance
This factsheet complies with FCA COBS 9.2 disclosure requirements for packaged products. The fund operates under UCITS regulations, providing investor protection through segregation of assets and daily liquidity. Risk warnings include potential capital loss from interest rate movements, inflation erosion of real returns, and concentration risk from UK sovereign exposure.

Market Outlook
Current positioning reflects our cautious stance on UK monetary policy, with the Bank of England's base rate at 4.75%. We maintain a bias toward medium-term maturities (5-15 years) to capture yield while managing duration risk. Recent additions include the 4.25% Treasury Gilt 2032 and 3.75% Treasury Gilt 2030, acquired during the December gilt auction programme.

Suitability Assessment
This product aligns with investment objectives prioritising capital preservation over growth. Typical investor profiles include retirees seeking income, trustees managing conservative mandates, and institutional investors requiring sterling duration exposure. The fund serves as an effective diversification tool within balanced portfolios, exhibiting low correlation (0.23) with UK equities over five years.

Liquidity and Dealing
Daily dealing is available with same-day settlement for orders received before 12:00 GMT. The fund maintains 3-5% cash weighting to meet redemption requirements without forced selling. Bulk redemption procedures apply for single withdrawals exceeding £10 million to protect remaining unitholders' interests.

Conclusion
The Meridian UK Gilt Fund continues to deliver on its conservative mandate through disciplined security selection and active duration management. Current market conditions favour high-quality government debt, supporting our constructive outlook for 2025 returns within the 3.5-4.5% range.
